declarations.description:
Purpose: A table to display data in rows and columns with advanced features like sorting, pagination, row selection, and expandable rows.
Category: Data Display, Layout
When to use: When you need to present tabular data with advanced interaction capabilities.
When not to use: For simple lists or when a grid layout is more appropriate.
Works with components: mc-avatar, mc-badge, mc-button, mc-checkbox, mc-icon, mc-input, mc-multi-select, mc-pagination, mc-popover, mc-select, mc-tooltip
Alternative component names: Data Grid, Data Table, Tabular View
declarations.name: McTable

slots.description: When you want to pass other mc-components or your own custom components to the table cell. The first parameter in the slot name {rowId} is a row id(the component will automatically try to access an \`id\` attribute, you can change that by passing the \`datakey\` attribute), the second {columnId} is the id of the column where custom component should be rendered, i.e. 10_status, 11_status, 12_status, etc
slots.name: `{rowId}_{columnId}`

slots.description: Header slot for a column with {columnId} as an \`id\` property.
slots.name: `{columnId}_header`

slots.description: Footer slot for a column with {columnId} as an \`id\` property.
slots.name: `{columnId}_footer`

slots.description: Slot for an expandable row content.
slots.name: `{rowId}_expanded`

slots.description: Slot in the table body. Can be used to display state of the table, i.e. loading, empty, error, etc.
slots.name: `state`

members.name: controller
members.default: new Controller(this, { data: this.data || [], columns: this.computedColumns, enableSorting: !this.sortdisabled, enableSortingRemoval: false, // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any enableRowSelection: (rowData: Row<Record<string, any>>): boolean => this.enableRowSelection(rowData), enableHiding: true, manualSorting: this.sortmanual, manualPagination: this.paginationmode === 'dynamic', enableColumnResizing: this.columnresize, columnResizeMode: this.columnresize ? 'onChange' : undefined, columnResizeDirection: this.columnresize ? 'ltr' : undefined, getCoreRowModel: getCoreRowModel(), getPaginationRowModel: undefined, getSortedRowModel: this.sortmanual ? undefined : getSortedRowModel(), getExpandedRowModel: this.expand ? getExpandedRowModel() : undefined, getRowId: (rowData: unknown): string => rowData[this.datakey]?.toString(), getSubRows: (rowData: unknown): unknown[] | undefined => rowData['children'], // prettier-ignore getRowCanExpand: (rowData: Row<Record<string, any>>): boolean => this._expandable.has(rowData.id.toString()) || this.hasChildrenData(rowData.original), // eslint-disable-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any state: { sorting: this._sort, pagination: this._pagination, rowSelection: this._rowSelection, expanded: this._expanded, columnPinning: this._pinnedColumns, }, onSortingChange: (sorting): void => { const newSort = doUpdate(this._sort, sorting); this._sort = newSort; // Clear span caches when sorting changes this.spanCache.clear(); this.skipRenderCache.clear(); this.controller.update({ state: { sorting: newSort }, }); if (!isServer && this._afterFirstUdpdate && this.shouldDispatchSortEvent) { this.dispatchEvent( new CustomEvent<IMcTableSortChangeDetail>('sortchange', { detail: { column: newSort[0]?.id, direction: newSort[0]?.desc ? 'descending' : 'ascending', sortedData: this.controller.table.getSortedRowModel().flatRows.map((row) => row.original), }, }), ); } }, onRowSelectionChange: (selection): void => { const oldSelection = { ...this._rowSelection }; const newSelection = doUpdate(this._rowSelection, selection); this._rowSelection = newSelection; this.controller.update({ state: { rowSelection: newSelection } }); if (!isServer) { this.dispatchSelectedOrExpandedEvents(oldSelection, newSelection, 'selection'); if (!this.selected?.length && !this._afterFirstUdpdate) return; this.dispatchEvent( new CustomEvent<unknown[]>('selectchange', { detail: this.controller.table.getSelectedRowModel().flatRows.map((row) => row.original), }), ); } }, onExpandedChange: (expansion): void => { let oldExpansion: Record<string, boolean>; if (this._expanded === true) { oldExpansion = Object.fromEntries(this.controller.table.getRowModel().rows.map((row) => [row.id, true])); } else { oldExpansion = { ...this._expanded }; } const newExpansion = doUpdate(this._expanded, expansion); this._expanded = newExpansion; this.controller.update({ state: { expanded: newExpansion } }); if (!isServer) { this.dispatchSelectedOrExpandedEvents(oldExpansion, newExpansion, 'expansion'); if (!this.expandopened?.length && !this._afterFirstUdpdate) return; this.dispatchEvent( new CustomEvent<unknown | undefined>('expandchange', { detail: this.controller.table .getExpandedRowModel() .flatRows.filter((row) => Object.keys(this._expanded).includes(row.id)) .map((row) => row.original), }), ); } }, onPaginationChange: (pagination): void => { const currentPagination = this._pagination; const newPagination = doUpdate(this._pagination, pagination); this._pagination = newPagination; this.controller.update({ state: { pagination: newPagination } }); if (!isServer) { //if the pagination is the default one and it's the first update, don't dispatch the event. This is to avoid double event dispatch on initial render. if ( !this._afterFirstUdpdate && currentPagination.pageIndex === DEFAULT_PAGINATION_PAGE_INDEX - 1 && currentPagination.pageSize === DEFAULT_PAGINATION_PAGE_SIZE ) { return; } this.dispatchEvent( new CustomEvent<Record<string, string | number | boolean | object>[]>('pagechange', { detail: this.getVisibleRows(), }), ); // Dispatch page size change event if page size changed const pageSizeChanged = currentPagination.pageSize !== newPagination.pageSize; if (pageSizeChanged) { this.dispatchEvent( new CustomEvent<number>('paginationpagesizechange', { detail: this.paginationpagesize, }), ); } // Only dispatch page change if page index actually changed (not just page size) const pageIndexChanged = currentPagination.pageIndex !== newPagination.pageIndex; if (pageIndexChanged) { this.dispatchEvent( new CustomEvent<number>('paginationpagechange', { detail: this.paginationcurrentpage, }), ); } // Scroll to top after DOM update if (this.paginationscrolltotop) { this.updateComplete.then(() => { if (this.tableWrapper) { this.tableWrapper.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' }); } }); } } }, })

members.name: isElementCurrentlySticky
members.description:
Determines if an element is currently affecting the layout at the top of the page
This includes both sticky elements in their sticky state and fixed elements positioned at the top

members.name: getStickyElementHeight
members.description:
Gets the effective height of an element only if it's currently affecting the top layout
This includes both sticky and fixed elements. Returns 0 if the element doesn't contribute to offset.

members.name: measureHeaderStickyOffset

parameters.description: Elements to check for sticky state changes (currently unused)
parameters.name: _targets

members.description:
Determines if any of the observed elements changed their sticky state
Used to optimize updates and only recalculate when necessary

members.name: getHeaderSlotPopover
members.description: Returns the mc-popover element passed via the header slot

type.text: { rowspan?: number; colspan?: number; shouldRender: boolean }

members.name: getTfootCellRenderer
members.description:
Returns a rendering function for a table footer cell.
It encapsulates the colspan state to skip rendering of the cells falling within a colspan.

type.text: { value: number | undefined; unit: string | undefined }
Array<Record<string, string | number | boolean> | string> | null

members.name: getVisibleRows
members.description: Returns visible rows, depending if pagination is enabled or not.

members.name: resetRowSelection
members.description: Resets the row selection to empty state

events.name: columnresizestart
events.description: Dispatched when user starts resizing a column.

events.name: columnresizing
events.description: Dispatched continuously (animation frame throttled) while a column is being resized.

events.name: columnresizeend
events.description: Dispatched when user ends resizing a column.

events.name: rowmouseenter
events.description: Dispatched when the cursor enters a row.

events.name: rowmouseleave
events.description: Dispatched when the cursor leaves a row.

events.name: hidecolumnschange
events.description: Dispatched when a column is hid/shown with the current hidecolumns state as detail.

events.name: rowclick
events.description: Dispatched when a row is clicked.
Dispatched when sorting direction on a column changes with a column id and direction as detail.
events.name: sortchange

events.description: Dispatched when a row is selected/deselected with the current selection state as detail.
events.name: selectchange

events.description: Dispatched when a row is expanded/collapsed with the current expansion state as detail.
events.name: expandchange

events.description: Dispatched when the page changes (only when pagination is enabled) with the visible rows as detail.
events.name: paginationpagechange

events.description: Dispatched when the page size changes.
events.name: paginationpagesizechange

events.description: Dispatched when a row is selected.
events.name: rowselected

events.description: Dispatched when a row is deselected.
events.name: rowdeselected

events.description: Dispatched when a row is expanded.
events.name: rowexpanded

events.description: Dispatched when a row is collapsed.
events.name: rowcollapsed

declarations.name: translations

declarations.description:
Table translations combining pagination labels with table-specific labels
This reuses all pagination labels (previous, next, pagesize, etc.) and adds table-specific ones
